project.title: Identification of phosphorylation sites of purified ORP8 from in vitro AMPK kinase assay
project.projectDescription: We investigated the potential role of AMPK in the phosphorylation of ORP8.In vitro kinase assay using purified ORP8 and kinase-active AMPK complex confirmed that ORP8 is the direct substrate of AMPK. Phosphorylated ORP8 from in vitro kinase assay was analyzed by HPLC-MS/MS.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Purified ORP8 protein (0.5 μg) in kinase assay buffer (60 mM Tris, 10 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM Na3VO4, 5 mM β-Glycerophosphate, 5 mM DTT and 100 μM AMP) was incubated with kinase-active AMPK complex (0.2 μg) (SignalChem) at 30oC in the presence or absence of 100 μM ATP or 50 μM compound C for 1 h. SDS sample buffer was then added to terminate the reaction. ORP8 from the in vitro kinase assay was separated using SDS-polyacrylamide gel electrophoresis and stained with Coomassie blue. The band of ORP8 was cut down and performed in-gel digestion with trypsin at 37oC overnight. After desalting, digested peptides were analyzed by HPLC-MS/MS.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The acquired MS/MS data were analyzed against a UniProtKB human or E.coli containing target proteins using Proteome Discoverer 2.4 or PEAKS studio X. Precursor mass tolerance was set to ±20 ppm and fragment mass tolerance was set to ±0.2 Da. Carbamidomethylation of cysteine (+57.021 Da), oxidation of methionine (+15.995 Da) and phosphorylation of Serine\threonine\tyrosine was set as variable modifications. All identified proteins had a FDR of ≤1%, which was calculated at the peptide level.

labPIs.title: Dr
labPIs.affiliation: Department of Biochemistry and Department of Metabolic Medicine of the Fourth Affiliated Hospital, Zhejiang University School of Medicine, Hangzhou 310058, China
labPIs.name: Wei Liu

references.referenceLine: Pu M, Zheng W, Zhang H, Wan W, Peng C, Chen X, Liu X, Xu Z, Zhou T, Sun Q, Neculai D, Liu W. ORP8 acts as a lipophagy receptor to mediate lipid droplet turnover. Protein Cell. 2023 14(9):653-667

Comment[Separation]: capillary reversed-phase C18 column (15 cm in length, 100 μm ID × 360 μm OD, 3 μm particle size, 100 Å pore diameter)
Comment[FlowRateChromatogram]: Analysis was performed at a flow velocity of 300 nL/min
Comment[GradientTime]: with a 180 min HPLC gradient from 0 to 100% of 0.1% formic acid in an acetonitrile buffer
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: Precursor mass tolerance was set to ± 20 ppm
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: fragment mass tolerance was set to ± 0.2 Da
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: An Orbitrap analyzer was used to perform a full-scan MS spectra survey with a resolution of r = 70,000 at m/z 400.
